m	Z	m

    Encapsulates a VCS-specific revision to install, along with any VCS
    install options.
    Instances of this class should be treated as if immutable.
    N

        Return the revision string that should be used in a requirement.

get_revision)
get_requirement_revision'
z'VersionControl.get_requirement_revision)
        Return the requirement string to use to redownload the files
        currently at the given repository directory.
        Args:
          project_name: the (unescaped) project name.
        The return value has a form similar to the following:
            {repository_url}@{revision}#egg={project_name}

get_src_requirement.
z"VersionControl.get_src_requirementr^
        Return the base revision arguments for a vcs command.
        Args:
          rev: the name of a revision to install.  Cannot be None.
        N
NotImplementedErrorr\
z VersionControl.get_base_rev_args)
destr 
        Return true if the commit hash checked out at dest matches
        the revision in url.
        Always return False, if the VCS does not support immutable commit
        hashes.
        This method does not check if there are local uncommitted changes
        in dest after checkout, as pip currently has no use case for that.
        Fr'
is_immutable_rev_checkoutP
z(VersionControl.is_immutable_rev_checkout)
        Return a RevOptions object.
        Args:
          rev: the name of a revision to install.
          extra_args: a list of extra options.
        r_
VersionControl.make_rev_options)
repor 
        posix absolute paths start with os.path.sep,
        win32 ones start with drive (like c:\folder)
        )
splitdriver
bool)
drive
tailr'
_is_local_repositoryj
z#VersionControl._is_local_repository)
netlocr&
        Parse the repository URL's netloc, and return the new netloc to use
        along with auth information.
        Args:
          netloc: the original repository URL netloc.
          scheme: the repository URL's scheme without the vcs prefix.
        This is mainly for the Subversion class to override, so that auth
        information can be provided via the --username and --password options
        instead of through the URL.  For other subclasses like Git without
        such an option, auth information must stay in the URL.
        Returns: (netloc, (username, password)).
        )
NNr'
get_netloc_and_auths
z"VersionControl.get_netloc_and_auth)
        Parse the repository URL to use, and return the URL, revision,
        and auth info to use.
        Returns: (url, rev, (username, password)).
        r
zvSorry, {!r} is a malformed VCS url. The format is <vcs>+<protocol>://<url>, e.g. svn+http://myrepo/svn/MyApp#egg=MyApp
zyThe URL {!r} has an empty revision (after @) which is not supported. Include a revision after @ or remove @ from the URL.rZ

        first fetch from the repository.

        Nr

        Args:

        Nr

        Install or update in editable mode the package represented by this
        VersionControl object.
        :param dest: the repository directory in which to install or update.
        :param url: the repository URL starting with a vcs prefix.

stdout_onlyr 
        Run a VCS subcommand
        This is simply a wrapper around call_subprocess that adds the VCS
        command name, and checks that the VCS is available
        )
Cannot find command z
 - do you have z
 installed and in your PATH?z
No permission to execute z
 - install it locally, globally (ask admin), or check your PATH. See possible solutions at https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/reference/pip_freeze/#fixing-permission-denied.N)

run_commandi
VersionControl.run_command)
        Return whether a directory path is a repository directory.
        z
Checking in %s for %s (%s)...)
join)
z&VersionControl.is_repository_directoryc
        Return the "root" (top-level) directory controlled by the vcs,
        or `None` if the directory is not in any.
        It is meant to be overridden to implement smarter detection
        mechanisms for specific vcs.
        This can do more than is_repository_directory() alone. For
        example, the Git override checks that Git is actually available.
        N)
z"VersionControl.get_repository_root)
